segments.text: Open Q&A this week. The theme in the questions is adoption: how do you get AI workflows to actually stick across a team, not just with the one champion?
First question. Someone asks: my team wants to level up but doesn't know where to start. My answer is to build skills as a team ritual, one small reusable skill per week, reviewed together.
When you build skills that way, the workflow stops living in one person's head. It becomes shared infrastructure, and that's the difference between a demo and adoption.
Second question is about tooling. Yes, you can connect internal systems with MCP servers so the whole team hits the same data through the same skills.
Into the unedited half. A member asks how to keep spend under control at scale. This is the cost optimization conversation everyone eventually has.
The levers are: cache aggressively, keep context lean, and pick the smallest model that clears the bar. Most teams overspend on context they never needed.
Last one is a deeper MCP servers question about auth. I'll walk through how we scope tokens so an agent only ever sees the data that particular workflow needs.
